## [00:00 - 03:03]

Okay, so now I just wanted to share a quick ninja trick with you. And this one's, I don't think anyone out there is doing this. It's actually sort of hard to tell if they're doing it, but I've watched, I haven't seen anyone doing this. And basically it's like, this happens, this is a list management, a way you can manage your list. And if you, so let's just say this is your email sequence. And, you know, this is where someone joins your list. And here's the first message, second message, third message, fourth, and so on. Okay, and let's just say you send in the first, you know, this goes on day zero when they first joined. This goes on day one, this goes on day two, three, four, seven, ten, and so on. So we'll just, we're running out of space there. So, okay, now, so you're, this is your, you've got your garden variety squeeze page here. And people join that squeeze page and they go here and then they just go right through that sequence. Right? Okay, now say you wanted to do a tele-seminar or something. Someone's, you know, you're going to interview someone on a tele-seminar and you put together this promotion for this tele-seminar. So this is your normal squeeze page. Then this is your tele-seminar squeeze page. Now, let's just say in this case, you, you want these people to get on your list. But when they join this tele-seminar and say that, you know, they, they sign up and the tele-seminar starts like, you know, say it's Tuesday and the tele-seminar is going to be Thursday. And, you know, so basically these people have to get this message that, okay, you know, they have to get the tele-seminar details message. And then they have to get the 24 hour reminder message and then they have to get the 60 minute reminder message. And then they have to get the replay message. Okay? So they need a whole bunch of different messages. Someone sends up your tele-seminar. You're going to have to probably send them at least four different emails. So you can't just drop them. You can't take these people and put them here on day zero and have them start getting your normal auto-respond your messages, right? It just doesn't, I mean, it doesn't work because they have to get a different sequence. But you don't want to go and start a new list every time. You know, you might do 10 tele-seminars until you don't want 10 different lists for 10 different tele-seminars. Okay, so this is what you do. This is the magic secret trick. Is what is you... So let's just say, you know, these are the days, but let's go ahead and erase that and say right here, we're going to, instead of putting those, the day sequence, this is just message one, two, three, four, five, six. So you've got six messages programmed in.

## [03:03 - 06:06]

Okay, so then what you do is you create, you create message seven, message eight, message nine, message ten. Okay, now each one of these messages have a delay. Like this one, you say, send this out one day after this message. And this one, you say, send it, send this out one day later. And this one, you say, send out one day later. And this one, you say, send out two days later. And this one, you say, send out three days later. And this one, you say, send out four days later. Does that make sense? So like, people, when they join, they get message one. They get, actually, this is actually the first message, the zero message. They get that message. Then they get this message one day later. They get the second message a day later. They get the third message a day and so on. So what you do here is this message, you set this for a nine hundred and ninety nine day delay. And then you set this for like, whatever, a one day delay and then a one day delay and then a one day delay. And now when someone joins this list, you can actually say what message to start them on. That's one of the parameters. When you have someone join your list, when they go through this form, you say have them join on message seven. Now this nine hundred and ninety nine day delay means the people here, they have to sit on this message for three, for almost a thousand days, for three years before they get this message. So these people aren't going to get that message. But these people, you're starting on message seven. And then the next day they're going to get this message and the next day they're going to get this message and the next day they're going to get that message. So basically what you did was this is sort of a ninja way to add people into a different segment of the list. And if you wanted, you could build out a whole, you could build out fifty messages here. And you could, and it's almost like you're running two different lists, two different auto responders. And it's very, very cool. Now after, let's say you go through this and you know, then they get this twenty four hour message, they get the sixty minute message, and they get the replay message. You send them all these messages and now it's a week or two weeks after the, after the tele-seminar. And you've got all these people sitting out here on message ten. Then you just get a whole to profile of support and you say, hey, could we take everyone that's on message ten and now put them back here. I'm message one and they'll do that for you. This isn't something you can do automatically in your control panel, but you can add support and you can have them move them back over here to message one. Now I know this looks sort of complicated. If you think about the logic, it's really not that crazy and it's a way you could do a whole series of promotions, whether they're tele-seminars or some other type of promotion and add people on the end of the list.

## [06:06 - 07:18]

And in fact, you could have, you could do the same thing multiple times and I have done this. You know, say you're going to run another tele-seminar, you could say message eleven is a nine hundred ninety nine day delay and twelve is a one day delay and thirteen is a one day delay. So now you've got, you know, another tele-seminar squeeze page and now that's driving people here and here and here. Okay, and it's, you could have multiple. I mean, you definitely want to keep track of what you're doing, but you can always then call a Webber and have them take these people and put them here. So it's a way of adding people onto your list and giving them a special sequence. It could be just a special welcoming sequence, you know, whatever the sequence you want. You can broadcast to these people or you can have sequences going out to them and it's very, very effective, but you're still putting them on the same list and you don't have to create like a bazillion different lists. So I just wanted to, I've actually never shared that one publicly before. So I figured out on my own, I don't think anyone's doing it. Certainly not seeing anyone teaching it. It's a very, very cool way to use Profile.
